strategy: Buy $8/$12 Call Spread — Sep 2026 (enter AFTER May 13 earnings)

wheelStrategy.wheelFlag: WHEEL-UNSUITABLE: Marvell cancelled all purchase orders April 27 after CFO NDA breach via Stocktwits (disclosed confidential Celestial AI shipping details). 4 securities law firms filed class action investigations. Management credibility damaged. Assignment = holding stock under active litigation cloud.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering premium-selling positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: Daily chart, 20-period 2σ Bollinger Bands. Lower BB estimated ~$5.50–7.00 post-crash volatility. Stock is still finding support after -53% crash from $15.50 ATH. Bollinger bands are wide and unreliable in crash recovery phase — wait for consolidation before using BB entry signal.

csp.annualisedYield: ~50–100% annualised on capital at risk (extreme post-crash IV)
csp.exitRule: Close at 50% premium captured. CRITICAL: Close before May 13 earnings AND before June 26 redomicile vote. Both events are IV-inflating binary risks.
csp.note: ⚠️ WHEEL-UNSUITABLE FLAG ACTIVE — See wheelFlag above. If entering despite flag: low capital at risk ($550–$650/contract), size to 1–2 contracts max. $463M cash gives fundamental floor. Assignment at $5.50–6.50 is below current price but you'd be holding stock under active securities litigation.

coveredCall.exitRule: Close at 50% premium. Close before May 13 earnings and June 26 vote.
coveredCall.note: If assigned, sell CC at $8–9 above assignment price. IV remains elevated post-crash — CC premium is attractive. Wait for litigation overhang to clear before sizing up.

recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — Enter POST-Earnings AND POST-Redomicile Vote
recommendation.entryNote: TWO events to wait out: (1) May 13 Q1 earnings — IV crush incoming; (2) June 26 redomicile vote — PFIC status overhang clears if shareholders approve U.S. redomicile. Enter after BOTH for cleanest entry. Use spread not naked call: IV at 71–95% makes naked calls expensive. Size very small (1% portfolio max) — CFO's Stocktwits NDA breach damaged management credibility and 4 law firms are circling. The $463M cash is the thesis anchor: zero dilution risk, 25+ year runway, and Semtech/LITEON/Sivers partnerships remain intact after Marvell exit.

exitPlan.label: Spread 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $9.50–10 (+29–36%) on Marvell replacement customer

exitPlan.label: Spread 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $11–12 (+50–63%) on production order confirmation

companyOverview: POET Technologies has developed the Optical Interposer — a patented semiconductor platform that monolithically integrates lasers, modulators, detectors, and electronics onto a single chip using standard wafer-level fab processes. The commercial payoff: photonic modules built from semiconductor economics rather than artisanal manual assembly, enabling dramatically lower cost and higher yield for 400G/800G/1.6T/3.2T optical interconnects that AI data centers require. Partners include Semtech (1.6T receivers), LITEON (AI module co-development), Sivers Semiconductors (light engines), and Quantum Computing Inc. (3.2 Tbps CPO engines). The stock peaked at a NASDAQ ATH of $15.50 on April 24, 2026 on AI photonics enthusiasm and the initial production orders — then crashed 50% in days. The cause: CFO Thomas Mika posted on Stocktwits revealing confidential shipping details about customer Celestial AI (acquired by Marvell for up to $5.5B), breaching their NDA. Marvell cancelled all purchase orders on April 27. Four law firms immediately filed securities class action investigations. Despite the commercial setback, the fundamental position is unusual: $463M cash against $1.3M/month burn rate = 25+ year runway with zero dilution needed. The technology is real (demonstrated Blazar platform at OFC 2026), and most partnerships remain active.

financials.note: -53% from ATH in 9 days on NDA breach + securities litigation
Q4 inflated by $30.6M non-cash warrant fair value adjustment
At current burn rate; zero dilution risk near-term
~12.4M shares; 0.21 days to cover — no squeeze setup
Call May 14 at 7:00 AM ET — wait to enter
Avg target $7.80–$8.40; 24/7 Wall St: $10.51 — thin coverage

catalysts: May 13 Q1 2026 earnings — first post-Marvell update; market needs to see a replacement customer pipeline or confirmation that other orders are accelerating
June 26 redomicile vote — shareholder approval of U.S. redomicile eliminates PFIC tax status overhang for U.S. shareholders (major structural positive)
$463M cash with $1.3M/month burn — 25+ year runway eliminates dilution fear; POET can outlast competitors and wait for market timing without capital constraints
Semtech 1.6T receiver and LITEON AI module partnerships remain intact after Marvell exit — the Marvell/Celestial AI order was ONE customer in a multi-partner pipeline
Lessengers 1.6T 2xDR4 transceiver samples (Q2 2026) — next near-term commercial milestone
OFC 2026 Blazar platform demonstration — industry validation of next-gen technology at the sector's leading conference
800G optical engine production ramp (Q3 2026) — revenue inflection point if manufacturing scales as guided

AI/data center structural demand: 400G → 800G → 1.6T → 3.2T upgrade cycle drives 35.9% CAGR for CPO/NPO modules through 2031; POET's ELS technology addresses the hardest integration challenge (laser integration)
risks: May 13 earnings: enter AFTER — IV at 71–95% will crush post-announcement; Q1 revenue will be minimal
CFO NDA breach (Stocktwits disclosure) — severe management credibility impairment; CFO status post-breach unknown; may require leadership change
Securities class action: 4 law firms filed investigations; lead plaintiff deadline June 29, 2026; PFIC misrepresentation claims are the core allegation — legal settlement costs possible
PFIC status — until June 26 redomicile vote passes, U.S. shareholders face adverse tax treatment on gains; creates selling pressure
No near-term revenue catalyst: Marvell cancellation removed the only disclosed major production order; $1.07M FY2025 revenue shows the commercial ramp is still very early
P/S ratio 1,044x on FY2025 revenue — even reaching $10M revenue in 2026 would leave valuation at 112x trailing sales
Competition: Broadcom has 50,000+ CPO switches shipped; Marvell/Celestial AI is now building directly what POET was supplying; Ayar Labs (backed by AMD, Intel, NVIDIA) is a credible alternative
High retail-dominated options flow (near-zero put/call ratio) — speculative crowd without fundamental backing; any narrative shift causes outsized sell-off

dilution.summary: Dilution is NOT the risk here. $463M cash with $1.3M/month burn = 25+ year runway. Main risks are litigation and management credibility.

items.label: Jan 2026 $150M direct offering (done)
items.detail: Oversubscribed follow-on raised cash to ~$463M. The largest capital event that strengthened the balance sheet. Priced and absorbed — no ongoing overhang.

items.label: Q4 2025 $225M equity raise (done)
items.detail: Series of offerings in late 2025 funded the current cash position. Significant dilution occurred then but is now fully absorbed into the share count.

items.label: Warrants: 37.36M shares + 5.79M options
items.detail: ~43M total potential new shares = ~28% dilution overhang above current share count. Exercise depends on stock price recovery; at $7.35 current price many warrants are likely underwater or at-the-money. Monitor strike prices.

items.label: Future raises: very low probability
items.detail: At $1.3M/month burn and $463M cash, POET does not need to raise capital for 25+ years at current burn. The cash is the primary fundamental argument for the stock floor.
